philosophy: Simple APIs that solve real problems. Composition over configuration. Less is more.

propLimits.rule: Component APIs must be simple and memorable. Complexity is a bug.

constraints.name: Maximum Props
constraints.rationale: Miller's Law - humans can hold 7±2 items in working memory
constraints.action: If more than 7 props needed, split into multiple components or use composition

constraints.name: Maximum Booleans
constraints.rationale: Boolean props create exponential variant combinations (2^n)
constraints.action: Replace boolean combinations with variant enum prop

violations: Boolean flags that should be variants
Props that are rarely used (less than 10% of usages)

iconsAsContent.rule: Never add leftIcon, rightIcon, icon, prefix, or suffix props
rationale: Icons are content - they belong in children/slots

wrong.issues: Fixed positions, limits customization, adds 2 props

correct.benefits: Full control over styling, any position, no additional props

compoundComponents.principle: Prefer composition over configuration. Let consumers control structure.
compoundComponents.rule: Components should do one thing well and compose with others

wrong.issues: Inflexible structure, many props, cannot customize layout

correct.benefits: Full control, optional sections, custom layouts
Custom button order, additional buttons, complex content
Reusable parts, custom layouts, independent components

violations: title/subtitle/footer as props when structure varies

rejectionCriteria.rule: Say no by default. Every feature has a cost.

criteria.rejectIf: Used by less than 10% of users
criteria.rationale: Rare features complicate API for everyone
Hick's Law - too many choices slow decisions

criteria.rejectIf: Adds more than 5 props to component

criteria.rationale: If you cannot explain it simply, it is too complex
Multiple ways to do same thing confuses users

criteria.action: Optimize for common case, not edge cases

decisionProcess: Who needs this? What percentage of users?
What is the cost? (props, complexity, maintenance)
Can this be achieved with composition?
Does this make the common case harder?
Can I explain this in one sentence?

complexityBudget.rule: Add a feature = Remove a feature
rationale: Simple APIs are easier to learn and use

implementation.newFeature: Identify what to deprecate or simplify
implementation.refactoring: Actively reduce complexity, not just add features
implementation.review: Question every prop, every variant, every option

metrics: API Surface Area: Count total props across all components
Variant Count: Count total variants per component
Depth of Nesting: Measure prop object nesting levels

twoClickRule.rule: Primary user tasks must complete in 2 interactions maximum
application: Primary actions visible without expanding menus
Common workflows do not require drilling down
correct: List to Item to Edit (2 clicks)
wrong: Menu to Settings to Advanced to Feature (4 clicks)
Primary action buried in dropdown menu

levels.content: Essential features, main actions, key information

implementationPatterns.description: Primary action visible, dropdown for secondary

violations: No way to access advanced features (too hidden)
Advanced features at same prominence as basic (confusing)
No visual hierarchy between primary and secondary

checklist: Prop count: 7 or fewer props per component
Boolean props: 2 or fewer booleans, prefer variants
No icon props: Icons via children/slots, not props
Composition: Compound components for complex structures
Complexity budget: Adding something = removing something
Two-click rule: Core tasks in 2 or fewer clicks

decisionGuide.question: Should I add this prop?
decisionGuide.answer: Only if more than 10% of users need it AND total props remain at 7 or fewer

decisionGuide.question: Prop or composition?
decisionGuide.answer: Composition for structure, props for behavior

decisionGuide.question: Should this be visible?
decisionGuide.answer: Only if in top 20% of usage

guidingLaws.application: Limit to 7±2 items in any list or prop count
80% of users use 20% of features - optimize for common cases
Do not add props for hypothetical future needs
